title: The Trials
wikitext:
__NOTOC__ {{VoidedTheme}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:The Trials}} {{Background|file=Void.png}}
{{Collapse|initial= [[File:NORMAL VERSE MORE LIKE.png|300px|center|link=]] Millions of people are trapped in the Backrooms. This number continues to grow. The question still remains. Who will be the next person to triumph? Who will be the person to find the truth, and what will they do? Who will prevail and rule the world? There will be one day, All I know is that there will be one day, one fateful day, Where the trials will finally be bested. |final=|body=
The Incarnation of Hell: '''The Trials'''
{{Quote|The information stated below is fully based on the testimony of CD.|NORMALVERSE}} ==Description==
[[File:CEASE.png|555px|link=|]]

'''The Flame Within'''
''An area of eternal suffering.
An area of no escape.
Here lies the spirits of all the people who have attempted to triumph the level despite all odds. But yet, there is still nothing here. Only endless flame, endless pain, and the slight feeling that something else may be watching you.''
Sometimes called '''The Incarnation of Hell''', accounts of '''The Trials''' have echoed across the Backrooms due to their disturbing and evocative qualities. Upon entry to '''The Trials''', a perpetual red haze restricts one's vision to a few meters in front of them, except for a cruel, iron barricade which seems to elude the effects of the fog. Ascending it leads to the central part of '''The Trials''', and backtracking strangely leads to [[Level 0|The Lobby]]—regardless of how one entered—which is likely the most ideal scenario. Formidable barriers confine wanderers to this section, and it is unknown what lies beyond them. The main area embodies stereotypical portrayals of Hell, comprising an endless, fiery abyss. Impossibly, most survivors bear only second-degree burns and scars despite the extreme heat (~400 degrees Fahrenheit). This place is separated into an infinite number of sectors, marked by rows of eternally burning trees, as well as surging molten rivers. Much like [[The Void]], death is impossible, regardless of the corporeal damage one endures. When wanderers stay in the level for too long, their sanity will gradually decrease. Common symptoms of decreasing sanity are hallucinations, panic attacks, personality shifts, absence of joy, etc. The effects of insanity in '''The Trials''' seem to be different from the rest of the other levels. Wanderers become insane much faster, and symptoms spread abnormally quickly. The level surroundings also seem to warp from the insane's perspective. It is unknown why this happens only within this level, but some people theorize that a disease may lurk within the depths of '''The Trials'''. There have been many theories regarding the nature of this level. All the tales and rumors state that the level contains the key to the truth, the truth of the Backrooms. A way to be freed of the ropes that tie one down. These theories suggest that secret areas lie within the depths of the level, each containing clues on how to find the truth. Not many people get the chance to enter the level, and even fewer people have the opportunity to see the next day. Others will say that the level itself is a mundane illusion. Many people will purposely venture through the level, only to find out all the tales and stories were mere fabrications. The level is neither significant nor insignificant. Neither safe nor dangerous. It is just an endless stairwell. Just a level within the many levels that reside within the Backrooms. There is no psychological torture. There is no hidden meaning within the level. There is no "secret truth" within the confines of it. But people have yet to find out if this is the truth. ===Attached Discovery Logs=== {{Collapse|num=132124124124|initial=Open Discovery Log #1?|final=Close Discovery Log #1?|body=
This is what I have resorted to. Submitting to the darkness, never returning to the surface ever again. I shall fall into eternal slumber once more. The grandiosity of the '''The Embodiment of Hell''' was merely a facade. A burning wasteland. A one-way trip to the gates of hell. Nothing but silence. I finally stare into the red night sky, accepting the end of my journey with open arms as I fade away, lost to history. Lucifer, take me to your kingdom of flames. The end is nigh. An indestructible curse I must embrace. Cast your spells, set me aflame, and I shall be doomed to collapse into ashes, as I breathe my final breath. My vision lost. My hearing lost. My thoughts lost. I didn't even have time to hear myself scream as I am encased with a thick coat of crimson red. But amid all my suffering, I see a glimmer of hope in the distance, and so I reach for the stars, never to be seen again. I am finally free.
}} {{Collapse|num=94378005934|initial=Open Discovery Log #2?|final=Close Discovery Log #2?|body=
Many rumors have stated that the only way for people to access and discover the "true ending" of the Backrooms was through '''The Trials'''. And so, after many, many years of searching, I have finally reached the level that has been lost in time. Now, I have finally arrived at the burning forest of freedom. My mission was straightforward: to discover the ending that everyone has dreamt of. My gut told me there should be clues lying in the level that may lead me to the truth. I stare up. The fire seemed to go on and on. Could something be lying at the very end? Contemplating the infinite possibilities, I continued venturing into the burning hot plains. While walking, I noticed numerous figures on all sides of me. What was most peculiar to me was that when I tried to directly stare at them, they disappeared. But there was one just standing there like it was stalking me. And so I walked towards that figure, still wondering why it hadn't disappeared. Curiosity blinded me as I got closer and closer. Once I was finally able to take a closer look at it. It was a dead man covered in blood and flesh. Out of disgust, I tried to punch it, which was probably one of the worst decisions I've ever made. But suddenly, there was something wrong. Its lifeless head started bobbing, up and down, and up and down again. Then it let out a horrible screech as if it had been in agony for hundreds of years. Then there was only darkness. The whole world flashed before my eyes. White. Black. White. Black. Like a never-ending cycle. I saw a vent. I saw a hallway. I saw a tunnel. I saw a light. And as the situation continued to deteriorate, a shocking realization struck me. The Truth is real is real is real is real is real is real }}
===Additional Information=== Due to the sheer number of disappearances occurring within '''The Trials''', we have yet to find any additional information about the level. {{Collapse|num=1321223812324|initial=Are you sure about that?|final=The trials have just begun.|body= {{Fullscreen|show=> Entering the first trial...|hide=> Regret.|padding=2rem 15%|number=2|content= {{Collapse|num=13212238121324|initial= '''TRIAL ONE: NAVIGATE'''
The first trial is a test of navigation and instinct. To pass the first trial, you must trust your instincts and dive in blind. If you are destined with luck and fortune, then you shall be worthy of moving on to the next trial. If not, then a fate worse than death awaits you. Everything you have been through has led up to this moment. You will be trapped in a ventilation system and submerged in total darkness. You are forced to traverse through a never-ending maze, much like the ones you have experienced before throughout your journey. Surrounded in pitch black, you will have to crawl through a claustrophobic series of vents with little to no air circulation until you find an exit. while all of your sins slither up to you as you continue to wriggle in desperation. But beware, as creatures lie in obscurity, waiting for you to drop your guard, waiting for their chance to strike. The creatures that lurk strike a sense of familiarity. They have similarities to your kind but are mostly bodies of flesh. Their bulging eyes and drooling mouths make you run away in fear. Their scream. Their screams are the most frightening of all, shouting at you for not helping them, making you relive terrible nightmares that were long forgotten. And they only serve one purpose: to kill. However, if you regret, there is still a way back. If you choose to exit the trials, then you must do it fast. You have five minutes to find an opening within the vent system, with the countdown starting as soon as you enter the first trial. If you manage to find and go through an opening, then [[Level 1000|the cycle]] shall repeat once more. In the unfortunate event of failure, then there's no going back. '''Good luck.''' [[File:Ventvent.png|center]] |final=|body= {{Collapse|num=13212239324|initial= '''TRIAL TWO: ANSWER'''
The second trial is a test of knowledge and belief. To pass the second trial, you must remember all the tales you have heard up until this point of time, and figure out what is right and what is wrong. You must dig deep into history, and search for a code, to be worthy of continuing towards the third trial. The answer is not something about the true ending, nor something about the appearance of the level, instead, it is about the level's identity itself. You will be trapped in a small, metal room. A regular office chair, a circular table, and a flickering light on the ceiling are inside the room. In front of you is a gigantic metal door, with a keypad. If you input the correct code, the metal door will slowly open, leading you to the third trial. The code is 19 digits in total, and you have three chances to input the correct door. Think quickly and dig deep into your memories, as you are timed. However, if you cannot think of the answer, the light may have some tips, so pay extra attention to the flickering. But, what happens if you fail? A bomb will be placed in the middle of the table, along with a 10-minute alarm beside it. Interacting with the bomb or the timer will result in detonation. If you input the wrong answer three times, or if the 10-minute timer is up, the bomb will also detonate. There is no possible way to defuse the bomb or rig the timer, and the walls beside you cannot be destroyed as well. There is another way to pass the second trial, but it is perilous. There is a secret code that you can input into the keypad, that will cause it to break and explode. Inputting the secret code, and waiting until the 10-minute timer is over will cause you to teleport back to the first trial. If you complete the first trial once more, you are also allowed into the third trial. 922________________ [[File:Keypad.png|center]] |final=|body= {{Collapse|num=13212229324|initial= '''TRIAL THREE: SEARCH'''
The third trial is a test of risk and sacrifice. To pass the third trial, you must take the necessary risks to stay alive. You must traverse and act fast if you end up encountering something. In the third trial, you will be pushed to the limit physically and mentally. Survival skills and bravery are two essential things you must have if you want to be worthy of moving on to the fourth trial. You must also be extremely careful to surpass the challenges that this trial has to offer. Upon entering the third trial, you will be teleported back to the main part of '''The Trials'''. You will spawn at a random height and must decide which path you should go towards. After exploring and walking for a bit, you will start to encounter creatures, usually creatures that you have met along your journey. These entities will spawn behind you and constantly chase after you until either you have completed the third trial or have died. The longer you survive, the more creatures will chase after you. This cycle will continue until you have been pushed to the edge. When that happens, you will start to lose control of your body and mind, causing you to make irrational decisions and causing your physical and mental health to drop massively. But to pass this trial, you must make sacrifices. This includes your own life. If you continue to try and run from your pursuers, the only result is death. It would be best if you took a '''leap''' of faith, by taking your own life, to be worthy of moving on to the fourth trial. However, if you manage to go back where you first started, and somehow enter the first trial once again, then you will be forced to go through the dangers of completing the first and second trial once more. The system will break, and once you complete the second trial again, you will be kicked out of the trials and will be unable to enter the trials ever again. Hope. [[File:922(2).png|center]] |final=|body= {{Collapse|num=1354039324|initial= '''TRIAL FOUR: RUN''' The fourth trial is a test of speed and consistency. If you do not have the necessary speed and energy needed to be worthy of moving on to the fifth wall. You must quickly find and unlock a certain door within the fourth trial. The fourth trial is a testament to your luck and strength and is objectively more challenging than the first three trials. At this point in the trials, there is nothing that can guarantee your survival and no essentials that can help you pass these next few trials. You will be taken to the middle of an extremely long hallway. The hallway has an ethereal vibe and resembles a hallway from a hotel. On either side of the hallway are hundreds of thousands of doors. Each of these doors has a number on it, indicating whether it is the correct door or the wrong one. The only correct numbered door within this trial is "922". But you must find the door fast, as life-threatening saws are coming from either end of the hallway, closing in on you. But this is only the first step. Each door has a lock on it. When you first enter the fourth trial, you will notice a massive stack of keys in front of you. You must try and guess the correct key to unlock the correct door, a near-impossible feat. The appearance of these keys is extremely similar, so you cannot guess the correct one through its appearance or shape. If you accidentally unlock a different door, behind the door will be a brick wall stopping you from going through it. This broken wall is indestructible. If you manage to be lucky enough to unlock the right door with the right key, then you are worthy of moving on to the fifth trial. However, another way to move on to the fifth trial is by finding a secret door. This secret door has no lock and is labeled with a picture of an eye instead of a number. Once you go through this door, more saws will appear behind you and you will be forced to run through another long hallway. This hallway is rotten and abandoned, with flickering lights that disrupt your sight. If you manage to survive and reach the exit, you will advance to the fifth trial. Almost there. [[File:Robloxdoorsomgomg.png|center]] |final=|body= {{Collapse|num=9839324|initial= '''TRIAL FIVE: DECIDE''' The fifth trial is the simplest of them all. It is a test of your intuition and willpower. This is where you have the most choice. This is the trial that will finalise all your past decisions. You are faced with a difficult choice and must weigh the pros and cons to proceed, making a truly informed decision before proceeding to the sixth trial. Would you rather lose all of your progress and go back to [[Level 0|where you started]] or continue for what you will yield for your work? You will be placed within an empty, sterile, small white cube room. The room contains two doors, one on the left and one on the right; the left one will send you back to [[Level 0|The Lobby]], restarting your progress forever, but on the other hand saving you from what lies beyond the fifth trial, escaping the horrors you don’t ever want to endure again. The right one will send you to trial six, where you will keep progressing, though without any knowledge of what lies ahead. The decision is up to you. This trial is where you will have to relive all of your past decisions in the Backrooms. It is where you decide whether the whole journey was worthwhile or if it was a waste. This will be your final decision ever, before you move on and fall or succeed. Choose your fate. Tread lightly. Yet, there is no more catch. There is nothing else going on within the room. No alternate way of getting through. You will not be able to cheat through the trials anymore. It is just you and these two doors, waiting for you to decide. What will you do? [[File:Cc.webp|200px|thumb|center]] |final=|body= {{Collapse|num=69420|initial= '''TRIAL SIX: REMEMBER''' The sixth trial is the final test, testing your patience and perseverance. As you enter the trial, all your choices will flash before your eyes. You will overthink every little detail, and will need to search through all of those thoughts to find the ultimate truth - the gateway to the end. The answer you are seeking to find will be extremely difficult, pushing you to the limit physically and mentally. These thoughts will continue to bombard you and flood your mind. To find the truth means to fully tap into these thoughts. Upon arrival in trial six, one will find oneself in a narrow, concrete hallway that stretches on forever, lighted by dim white lights on the ceiling. You will be forced to walk through this hallway, and when you do, your choices will appear before your eyes, taunting you, as aforementioned. Once you walk forward, you will be unable to turn back. This is your finale. Whether you will succeed will depend on your will and effort. [[File:Ncnc.jpg|200px|thumb|center]] As you go on, the hallway will become increasingly distorted. The walls will drip with blood, and crimson red spots and lines will appear everywhere, clouding your vision. It is up to you to endure the pain and anguish. Up to you to continue walking, step by step. Any attempts to think of anything else are all fragmented. Will you succeed? Will you pull through after what felt like continuous pain? In the distance is a light. [[File:BURN.png|350px|thumb|center]] |final=|body= {{Collapse|num=3610|initial= {{Background|file=Empty.jpg}}
'''TRIAL SEVEN: COMPREHEND'''
You have reached the ending. You have succeeded in finding the truth. Trial seven is a reward for those who are deserving. For those who are worthy. You have earned your right to be in this room. But can you understand? The glimmering door lights the passageway to the truth. What will you do with this information? Will you use it for good? Will you use it for bad? Can you comprehend what is standing in front of you? |final=|body= }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Entities== There are no known entities within '''The Trials'''. {{Collapse|num=132122382904124|initial=Are you sure about that?|final=|body=

'''Realization.'''

Countless people explore the level, in hopes of finding the truth. The one true. But are there really that many people willing to sacrifice everything to find the truth? Or is it merely just a figment of your imagination? Are you sure that those you have seen were, '''people?''' Truth is a weird concept. To discover the truth is to achieve ultimate freedom and enlightenment, but in reality, truth is just a weapon. A weapon of manipulation and control. People would sacrifice riches, others, and even their own life for it. They would give up everything, just for truth. Doesn't that seem wrong? Immoral? And so, to avoid the use of this dangerous weapon, countless other beings take drastic measures to stop others from achieving this feat, taking their appearances and manipulating their feeble minds. They infect wanderers to increase their numbers, or if push comes to shove, murder them to stop the discovery of the truth. All to protect the sacred truth. And so even now, their numbers continue to grow, but there will be a day. A day when someone discovers the reality that lies behind these stone-cold walls. [[File:Shapeshifters.png|center]] }}
==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the nature of '''The Trials''', it is impossible for colonies or outposts to exist within the level. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== Hit rock bottom. ===Exits=== Access the truth. {{Collapse|num=17377373737373|initial=Open Author Credits|final=Close Author Credits|body= Author(s): [[User:NormalDudeWithaNormalLife|Normal]], [[User:PlutoniumOnInternet|Plutonium]], [[User:Cringeaccount123456789|Cringe]]
SPaG checker: [[User:YourUsualGlitch|Glitch]] }} }} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Anomalous Levels]]
